---
title: "Add External APT Repositories on Debian with `extrepo`"
subtitle: "Note#199"
id: "199"
date: 2026-03-10
date-modified: ""
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
On Debian (and derivatives), adding a third-party APT repository means fetching a GPG key, writing a `.list` or `.sources` file, and hoping the URL doesn't rot. `extrepo` handles all of that.

```bash
sudo apt install extrepo
```

## Usage

```bash
# List available repositories
extrepo search

# Enable a repository (adds key + source)
sudo extrepo enable sury

# Disable it (removes key + source)
sudo extrepo disable sury
```

## References

* [Debian Manpages - extrepo](https://manpages.debian.org/stable/extrepo/extrepo.1p.en.html)
* `man extrepo`
